displayTitle: What the Vision Exam Actually Checks

beats.narration: In visual testing, your eyes are the measuring instrument. So before you're certified, they get verified — just like any other instrument.

payload.title: VISION EXAMS
payload.sub: Your eyes are the instrument — and instruments get checked.

beats.narration: The near vision test is simple: read a chart of graduated text at a set close distance. The smallest line you read defines your acuity.
A typical requirement: Jaeger two, at about twelve inches, with at least one eye — corrected vision counts — and it's repeated at least annually.
When the method depends on it, you'll also prove color and gray shade perception — like reading numbers hidden in colored dot plates.

payload.heading: When the method depends on it

beats.narration: So think of it this way: the vision exam is the calibration sticker on the inspector. Keep yours current.

payload.sub: The vision exam is the calibration sticker on the inspector — keep it current.
